rationale: Title Y matches PACE's formal title-case structure with an explicit study-design label, clearly scoped outcome terms, and precise patient descriptor, while Title X introduces confusion with 'prior or future sternotomy' and lacks a design identifier.
loser_weakness: Title X's phrase 'prior or future sternotomy' is ambiguous and undermines clinical precision, and its sentence-case, label-free format does not align with PACE conventions.
